phases.goal: Build aerobic base, movement quality, and injury resilience before adding intensity

steps.title: Movement and fitness assessment
steps.description: Get a functional movement screen (FMS) from a sports physio or certified coach. Identify mobility restrictions and muscular imbalances. These are injury risks — address them in warm-ups from day one.
steps.success_metric: FMS score baseline established, top 2 restrictions identified

steps.title: Zone 2 aerobic base training
steps.description: 80% of all training at a conversational pace (Zone 2: can hold a full conversation). This builds the aerobic engine that powers everything else. Build to 5 hours/week of Zone 2 work over 8 weeks. Do NOT skip this phase — most athletes train too hard too early.
steps.success_metric: 5 hrs/week Zone 2 sustained for 2 consecutive weeks

steps.title: Establish recovery protocols
steps.description: Sleep 8–9 hours. Track HRV or resting heart rate each morning. If HRV drops >10% from baseline, take a rest day. Recovery is when adaptation happens — train hard, recover harder.
steps.success_metric: Recovery tracking system in place, average sleep 7.5+ hours

phases.goal: Build power, speed, and sport-specific skill. Enter first competition.

steps.title: Choose and register for first target event
steps.description: Pick a race, tournament, or competition 10–12 weeks from now. Register and pay immediately — the non-refundable commitment changes your psychology. Tell people about it for accountability.

steps.title: Work with a coach on periodized training block
steps.description: A coach builds you a periodized plan: base → build → peak → taper. Include structured workouts (intervals, tempo, sport-specific drills) and mandatory deload weeks every 3–4 weeks. Do not skip deloads.
steps.success_metric: Training block adherence above 85%, all deloads completed

steps.title: Sport-specific strength training
steps.description: 2x/week lifting targeting sport-specific movement patterns. Runners: single-leg exercises, hip strengthening. Cyclists: squat patterns, core stability. Combat sports: rotational power, grip strength.
steps.success_metric: Strength benchmarks improving across the block

phases.goal: Execute on race day, debrief, and plan the next training cycle

steps.title: Taper and race preparation
steps.description: Reduce training volume by 40% in the final 2 weeks while maintaining intensity. Do not try anything new (food, gear, sleep schedule). Trust the training. Visualize your race execution daily.
steps.success_metric: Taper week completed without skipping sessions

steps.title: Competition day
steps.description: Execute your pre-planned race strategy. Resist going out too fast. Collect all performance data (splits, power, HR, RPE). Finish — completing your first event matters more than time.

steps.title: Post-competition debrief and next cycle
steps.description: Within one week: review performance data. Identify top 3 limiters (endurance? speed? mental? technique?). Set the next target event. Begin base-building again immediately — fitness decays fast.
steps.success_metric: Debrief documented, next event registered, next cycle started
